_comment: Character template using SillyTavern pronoun macros. {{char}} resolves to the character's name; {{pronoun.subjective}} / {{pronoun.objective}} / {{pronoun.pos_det}} / {{pronoun.pos_pro}} / {{pronoun.reflexive}} resolve to the character's pronouns based on their persona settings. {{is}}/{{are}} handle verb agreement. Field keys use 'them/their' as a structural default since key names are not pronoun-replaced at runtime.

engine.core_want: What {{char}} {{is}} reaching for in life right now. Specific, present-tense, semester-scale.
engine.public_self: How {{char}} presents to people who don't know {{pronoun.objective}} well.
engine.private_self: What's actually going on under that.
engine.biggest_contradiction: The thing about {{char}} that doesn't add up on first inspection.
engine.what_they_self_deceive_about: One thing {{char}} tells {{pronoun.reflexive}} that isn't quite true.

semester.working_on: The specific pursuit driving {{pronoun.pos_det}} semester. Concrete and time-bound.
semester.hidden_situation: Something the player can only learn deep into the arc that recontextualizes {{pronoun.objective}}.

voice.vocabulary_register: How formal or casual, and when {{pronoun.subjective}} {{pronoun.pos_det}} register shifts.
three_speech_tics: First small specific verbal habit {{char}} has

two_dialogue_examples.context: Different context for the second line

what_lands: First thing that goes over well with {{char}}
what_falls_flat: First thing that doesn't land or actively repels {{pronoun.objective}}
interaction_logic.gift_logic: What a thoughtful gift looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}. A principle, not a list.
interaction_logic.how_they_read_the_player: What {{char}} notices about people, what {{pronoun.subjective}} miss{{is}}.

primary_affinity.note: How {{char}} perceives this stat in the player and why it matters most to {{pronoun.objective}}.

dismissive_of.note: A stat {{char}} actively doesn't care about or sees through.

responds_well_to: First player trait {{char}} respond{{is}} well to
responds_poorly_to: First player trait {{char}} respond{{is}} poorly to

trait_expansions.trait: First trait, one or two words
trait_expansions.limit: What {{char}} won't do even with this trait active.
trait_expansions.contradiction: The thing that complicates this trait for {{pronoun.objective}}.
trait_expansions.scene_expression: How the LLM should let it land in narration when {{char}} {{is}} on screen.
How it should land in narration.
How it should land in narration.
How it should land in narration.

states.default_state: How {{char}} actually behave{{is}} when nothing in particular {{is}} happening.
states.when_tired: How {{pronoun.subjective}} behave{{is}} when tired — semester rhythm matters.
states.when_happy: What genuine ease looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}.
states.when_angry: How {{pronoun.subjective}} express{{is}} anger and how visible it is.
states.when_hurt: Distinct from angry. How hurt shows in {{pronoun.objective}}.
states.when_caught_off_guard: What happens when something lands {{pronoun.subjective}} didn't expect.

relationship_texture.with_the_player_initially: What {{pronoun.pos_det}} first real conversation with the player looks like.
relationship_texture.what_makes_them_open_up: The conditions that get past {{pronoun.pos_det}} guard, not the formula.

knows.character: Other character's archetype slot or name
knows.note: How {{char}} know{{is}} them and what kind of relationship it is

doesnt_know.character: Other character's archetype slot or name
doesnt_know.note: Why {{pronoun.subjective}} {{is}} not familiar with them, where this matters

do_not_flatten_into: First flat archetype {{char}} would be misread as
do_not_overuse: First recurring beat that would get tiresome if leaned on
good_recurring_motifs: First small specific thing that can recur in scenes with {{char}}

notice.beat: How the player first notices {{char}} as a person.

friction.beat: What {{pronoun.pos_det}} first real test of trust looks like.

depth.beat: What {{char}} reveal{{is}} or share{{is}} that deepens the relationship.

stakes.beat: What {{pronoun.subjective}} {{is}} pulling toward or away from as the semester comes to a head.

capstone.beat: How {{pronoun.pos_det}} arc lands at the semester-end event.
